lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there. Remind me, where can I find Commander Fullore?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: She'll be somewhere in the castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's all this about?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Ah, hello and welcome. I'm Martin Holt, leader of Kourend's Royal Society. This is our little protest against the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you, I'm [player name]. So, what's wrong with the Council?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Now there's a question! I have a better one. What's right with the Council?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I bet you didn't know that the Council was originally only temporary. After the last King died, they were only supposed to rule until his daughter came of age.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: However, according to the Council, the King's daughter went mad. Such a wonderful convenience for them of course. With her out of the picture, the Council became permanent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. What else don't you like about the Council?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Democracy! That's meant to be the big thing for the Council. Funny thing is, although they're meant to be elected by the people, the same four people have won every single election. Little odd, no?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is a bit suspicious, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Indeed it is. So, do you want to join our protest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now. But thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Oh my friend, suspicious is the dish of the day whenever the Council is around. And what I've just told you barely scratches the surface. Things have been getting even worse recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Do you know of Councillor Sophia Hughes? Lovely lady. Spends her free time with the Disciples of Yama, a wonderful cult of Zamorakian demon summoners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Councillor Hughes? I was the one who helped imprison her in the first place.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Ah, so you're the adventurer who's been cooking up a storm around here. Well you'll be pleased to know your hard work was all for nothing. Councillor Hughes has escaped. Lucky her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Escaped? How?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Believe it or not, no one actually knows. The Council is trying to keep it all on the down low of course. Almost like they have something to hide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's quite the accusation. Do you have proof?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Oh, we will soon! Just you give it time. They'll slip up sooner or later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Rather than just waiting for that to happen, maybe I could investigate her escape.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: What an interesting idea. I suppose you would be well placed to have a little nosey, considering your history with the Councillor. Though you'd need to get permission, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I do that?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I hear the Kourend Royal Guard are leading the investigation. Commander Lydia Fullore is their leader. You'll find her in the castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go speak to her.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Good luck friend. Your eyes are open now. Don't trust the Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I'll stay out of this one. Good luck with your protest.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: As you wish, but you shouldn't be afraid to open your eyes. If you change your mind, we'll be here.

sections.title: Talking to Commander Fullore again but before heading out

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Are you ready to head over to Councillor Hughes' home?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'm Commander Lydia Fullore, leader of the Kourend Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then you're just the person I'm looking for. I'm [player name]. I hear you're the one to talk to about the recent escape of Councillor Hughes.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Ah, let me guess, you've been talking to Martin Holt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know him?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Oh, I know him alright. Martin and I have quite the history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? He didn't mention it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: He wouldn't. He has a very selective memory. I imagine he also didn't mention that he used to work for the Council that he hates so much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... that's not something that ever came up.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well I imagine his Royal Society friends wouldn't make so much of him if they knew where his loyalties used to lie.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I'm guessing you don't agree with his views on the Council.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well I wouldn't be very good at my job if I did. Martin is always quick to point out the failings of the Council, but he conveniently forgets about all the good they've done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You know that smell in Port Piscarilius?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is a bit ripe there.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Imagine that but ten times worse. That's what it was like before the Council had a new sewage system installed. The entire kingdom is now cleaner and safer because of them.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Not only that, but without the Council, you wouldn't even be stood here. It was under them that trade with your lands to the east was established.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Without those trade routes, you would have no easy way of getting here.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I could go on, but I have more urgent things to attend to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like the escape of Councillor Hughes?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well, since it's clearly no secret, yes. The former Councillor is a dangerous individual, so I want her back in a cell as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I might be able to help you with that. In case you didn't know, I was the one who helped catch her in the first place.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: So, this is what Martin has put you up to! Well, no thank you. The last thing I need is one of his people getting in the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not one of his people! I barely know him. I'm just a willing adventurer who wants to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think about it. However this investigation goes, you'll be seen as biased towards the Council. I'm a neutral third party with no ties to the Councillor or the protesters. I'm in an ideal position to help out here.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Hmm... you do have the experience. And I can't deny, the fact that you have the support of the protesters could be useful.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This whole situation with Councillor Hughes has caused enough civil unrest. We certainly don't need it to get any worse.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Alright, you can help. But for the duration of this investigation, you're working for me. What I say goes, is that clear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Crystal clear. Where do we start?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Right now we don't have any leads on where Councillor Hughes might have gone. I'm heading over to her home to see if there's anything there that can help us. You should join me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's get going.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Commander Fullore! How wonderful to see you.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Tomas! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Why, I'm here about Sophia Hughes of course! As the person who discovered her corruption in the first place, I was an obvious choice to lead the investigation into her disappearance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that wasn't you! I did all the work!

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Ah, and hello again, my financially challenged friend! Of course, the capture of Sophia Hughes wouldn't have been possible without your minor contributions.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The Kourend Royal Guard are conducting the investigation, Tomas. Your presence is not needed.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well, that's just the thing. Councillor Andrews was so impressed with my work, he put me in charge of this investigation.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: That is not his call to make! I'm in charge of Council security. This investigation is mine to lead!

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: He is head of the Council. If he wants to make that call, he can.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Regardless...

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Look, I can see you're very passionate, which is wonderful. Tell you what, as you're so eager, I'll allow you both to help me in my investigation.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Do join me inside when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Captain Ginea. I'm guessing Lord Shayzien sent you.

lines.speaker: Captain Ginea
lines.text: He did. He's keen to see this Hughes business sorted quickly.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: He's not alone there, but he doesn't need to worry. We have things under control.

lines.speaker: Captain Ginea
lines.text: Forgive me Commander, but it doesn't seem like you do. Hughes escaped from right under your nose, and now Councillor Andrews is putting his faith in Lawry over you.

lines.speaker: Captain Ginea
lines.text: While this may be Council business, Lord Shayzien is responsible for dealing with both internal and external threats. He wants to be sure that Hughes won't be an issue.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You have my word. She'll be back in a cell before the day is out.

lines.speaker: Captain Ginea
lines.text: I hope you're right. There's already a lot of doubt in the Council these days. It would be rather embarrassing for them if the Shayzien Army had to step in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For someone who supports the Council so much, you don't seem very happy about Councillor Andrews' decision.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I can support the Council while not agreeing with every choice they make.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel like you're not totally being honest with me.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Would that be so wrong? I hardly know you.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Here's some advice for you: focus on the investigation. Leave the politics to the experts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I guess we'd better get started.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Have a look around the house and see if you can find any hints on where Councillor Hughes might have gone. I'll ask around out here and see if anyone's seen anything suspicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Good luck. Don't let Lawry get in your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just need to do something first.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Okay, just let me know when you're ready to depart.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt after beginning the investigation

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Hello again. I hope things are going well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad thanks.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Well, keep up the good work. After all, it would be very awkward for our friends on the Council if you caught Sophia Hughes again.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Hello again. Your desire to help out with my investigation is to be commended, but you really shouldn't worry. I'll have the former Councillor back in a cell in no time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you already have some leads?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Not just yet. It won't be long though. Not with my resourcefulness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... second only to your humility.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: How very kind of you. I should tell you though, a skillset like mine isn't gained over night. If you wish to become like me, it will take a lot of hard work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep that in mind. Anyway, I should get back to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found something that might be useful.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Oh yes? What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like the Councillor might be hiding out at Mount Quidamortem.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Mount Quidamortem? That's quite the trek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could go if you like?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: How very kind of you, but no need. This is my investigation, and I will handle it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sucker.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you say so.

sections.title: Talking to Commander Fullore in Kingstown

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I know where Councillor Hughes has gone.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Farming Guild.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Huh, not where I'd have expected. But maybe that's the point.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: If you think that's where she is, I'd recommend you go and check it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Where abouts is it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: North of the River Molch, west of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Deeper Lode.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Seems a risky place for her to go. I'd have thought she'd pick somewhere quieter.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: If you're sure though, I'd recommend you go and scout it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Where abouts is it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It's the pub in Lovakengj, north west of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Little Shop of Horace.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You don't think Horace is helping her, do you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well we can't make any assumptions.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: True. If that's where you think she is, I suggest you go and check it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Where abouts is it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It's the general store in Hosidius, south east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Chasm of Fire.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: That's where most of her cultist friends spend their time, seems a bit obvious.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I guess it could still be worth a look. If you think she's there, you should go and scout it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Where abouts is it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: On the outskirts of Shayzien, west of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well, keep looking around. There must be something here that can help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was the date when Councillor Hughes escaped?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It was two days ago, on the 26th of Fentuary.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... If she escaped two days ago, why are we only searching her house now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The start of the investigation was... delayed. I can't say any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know there's a demon upstairs?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: A demon? Well I guess the Disciples of Yama are known for that sort of thing. Still, that's going to be a nightmare to deal with. Can it get out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Good. Do your best to avoid it and we'll worry about it later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Have a look around the house and see if you can find any hints on where Councillor Hughes might have gone. I'll ask around out here and see if anyone's noticed anything suspicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. I'll let you know if I find anything.

sections.title: Talking to the sergeant by the Farming Guild entrance

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for Sophia Hughes.

lines.speaker: Sergeant
lines.text: Sophia Hughes? The councillor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. I think she might have come here at some point.

lines.speaker: Sergeant
lines.text: Well, if she did it wasn't recently. We'd have noticed a councillor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Horace
lines.text: Hello, I'm Horace. Welcome to my shop. Would you like to buy or sell something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Had any councillors shop here recently?

lines.speaker: Horace
lines.text: Not as far as I can recall. Councillor Hughes used to order things from time to time, before she was arrested. None of the others have ever shopped here though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Yama
lines.text: Welcome. Have you come to give your soul to the Chasm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Sophia Hughes.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Yama
lines.text: You are not alone in that, but you will not find her here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's one of your lot though, right? This seems like a good place for her to hide.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Yama
lines.text: You are welcome to go in and check. You will find nothing. Sophia Hughes no longer has a place here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, if you say so.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Hello, human. What's your pleasure today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So about the Councillor.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: You again? I've already told you everything. The woman mentioned Veos over at Port Piscarilius. She must be catching his ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes. I need to get there quickly.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Fuggy: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Hello, human. What's your pleasure today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Had any councillors stay here recently?

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Councillors? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the disappearance of Councillor Sophia Hughes. I have reason to believe she rented a bed here.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: A disappearing councillor? That's a new one. We don't really get many humans renting beds here. The only one recently was a woman who stayed here last night, but she didn't look much like a councillor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She could have been in disguise. Do you know where this woman is now?

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Well she left not long before you got here. I didn't really speak to her but I did hear her mention something about catching a ship at Port Piscarilius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A ship? She's trying to escape! Do you know which ship?

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Well let's see now...

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Blimy! That was a good one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor was that?

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Ah, not from around here are you? That was an earthquake. We get them here from time to time. I think they come from that volcano to the west, but I'm no expert.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Anyway, what were we on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Councillor.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Ah yes, the Councillor. I seem to recall her mentioning Veos. It must be his ship she's taking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get there quick. Thank you for the help.

lines.speaker: Fuggy
lines.text: Not a problem. Good luck with your search.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, where's Veos?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Herbert
lines.text: Your guess is as good as mine. He was here until that earthquake. Then he suddenly just wandered off, like he was in some sort of trance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what about his ship?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Herbert
lines.text: I guess I'll have to look after it until he comes back. I hope he won't be long. I'm not really cut out for this kind of thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a councillor.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Herbert
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe Councillor Sophia Hughes is planning to board this ship. I need to find her.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Herbert
lines.text: The one that got arrested? If she wants to board the ship she'll need to do it soon. We're nearly ready to cast off. I guess you could wait around and see if she shows up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a bad idea. I think I'll do just that.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Heard you were out here. Thought I'd come see how things are going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not spying on me are you?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Spying is a strong word. I just like to keep an eye on people I've not worked with before. Especially in a situation as delicate as this one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's one way of putting it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: So, what of Hughes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She booked passage on this ship, but she's not arrived yet. If she's coming, it will be soon.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: And we'll be here when she does. Not bad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Starting to trust me now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Don't get ahead of yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, looks like we might have a visitor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sophia Hughes. Going somewhere?

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: You! This is all your doing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The only person to blame is yourself. When you play with fire, you get burnt.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It's over, Sophia. You should come quietly.

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: Commander Fullore! He would send you, wouldn't he? The little lapdog of Councillor Andrews!

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: He knew, you know! They all did. They were more than happy with my work while it was secret. But now they betray me without a second thought, just to protect themselves!

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: You'd think they'd have some loyalty after all we did! After what we did to him. After what we did to his girl! We were in it together until it no longer suited them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about? What girl?

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: Don't think I'm coming quietly! I've fought too hard to stop now!

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: Judge of Yama, hear my voice! I call upon you to bring justice to the unworthy! Cleanse them in a sea of fire! Cleanse them in a sea of blood!

lines.speaker: Demonic Voice
lines.text: Sophia Hughes. You have called upon me. Those that oppose you will be judged in the name of Yama.

lines.speaker: Demonic Voice
lines.text: But know this, Sophia Hughes. Those that call upon the Judge must first be judged themselves.

lines.speaker: Demonic Voice
lines.text: You have betrayed the trust of Yama, Sophia Hughes. You have used his generosity to your own advantage. For this, your life is forfeit.

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: What? No!

lines.speaker: Demonic Voice
lines.text: Come. Serve your master one last time. Bathe yourself in a sea of fire. Bathe yourself in a sea of blood.

lines.speaker: Sophia Hughes
lines.text: No... Wait!

lines.speaker: Judge of Yama
lines.text: And so the justice of Yama has been brought upon Sophia Hughes, as it will be brought upon all who wrong him.

lines.speaker: Judge of Yama
lines.text: Lydia Fullore, [player name]. You have been declared enemies of Yama. Your actions have endangered the work of his children. For this, your lives are forfeit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! You can't just show up and start incinerating people. That's not how justice works. How about you clear off, before I make you!

lines.speaker: Judge of Yama
lines.text: [player name]. You have challenged the Judge of Yama. Your guilt will be determined through combat.

lines.speaker: Judge of Yama
lines.text: Should you succeed, you will be free of your sins. Should you fail, you will be bathed in fire. You will be bathed in blood.

lines.speaker: Judge of Yama
lines.text: Now, in the name of Yama, let us begin.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Here he is, the hero of the hour. On behalf of the Council, and indeed all of Kourend, thank you for dealing with this messy situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was nothing.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: You're far too humble. You've done so much for our kingdom in your short time here. Rest assured, we have noticed. In fact, I consider you a firm friend of the Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you managed to work out how Councillor Hughes escaped?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Not yet. You need not worry though, we'll get to the bottom of it. That said, I do have another ask of you... if you don't mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Commander Fullore tells me you've had dealings with Martin Holt and his group.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure about dealings, but we've met.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Well regardless, your handling of the situation with Sophia Hughes seems to have had quite an impact on them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I am quite impactful. So, what is this ask?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: This recent situation has been most unfortunate. People are angry, I understand that. That said, Sophia Hughes has been dealt with. It's time for Martin Holt and his protestors to go home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see. You want me to convince them.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: It would be greatly appreciated. Civil unrest is of no benefit to anyone. We'd all be better off putting this behind us.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Now, I must be off. Plenty of work to do. Good luck with Martin and his friends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We're looking into how Sophia Hughes might have escaped. I'll let you know if we find anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know that's not what I mean.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'm not sure what you're on about. Now, I'm sorry, but I need to get to work.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: *whispers* Meet me just south of the castle.

sections.title: Talking to Commander Fullore outside Kourend Castle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Councillor Hughes was a deranged cultist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And yet?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I don't know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you not know? Or do you just not want to admit it? The man and the girl she spoke of. What did she mean?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Ever since King Kharedst IV died, there have been those who have questioned his death. People like Martin.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It was all just wild speculation, though. The King was old, and he died of an illness. It happens all the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The man Councillor Hughes mentioned. It could have been the King she was referring to.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Maybe. Or maybe it was someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the girl she mentioned? The King had a daughter.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Rose. She was meant to take the throne after him, but she was too young. His advisors took over until she came of age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Council. It never went that way though, did it? She never took the throne. Martin said they declared her mad.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Grief can do a lot to someone. Rose and her father were very close.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Listen to us! Are we really considering this? The Council rule Kourend. They have done so successfully for 20 years now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to Rose in the end? Where did she go?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: After what happened, public life was no place for her. The Council arranged for her to move away so she could escape all of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is she now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I don't know...

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Look, I'm the commander of the Kourend Royal Guard. My duty is to the Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As commander of the 'Royal Guard' your duty is surely to royalty, not to some Council.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: What kind of person would I be if I betrayed them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of person would you be if you followed them blindly, even when all the evidence says not to?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on...

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I can't! But you can.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Councillor Andrews may have been all nice to you, but he's furious with me. He's blamed me for what happened in Port Piscarilius. I have no hope of doing anything now without him knowing.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: But you, on the other hand... You're just some adventurer as far as they're concerned. They won't be keeping an eye on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But where would I start?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Rose. If she's still alive, then she's the key to the truth. You have to find her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'm going to hate myself for this, but I know someone who could help.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Martin. He's leader of Kourend's Royal Society. He knows more about the last royal family than anyone. If anyone can help you find Rose, it's him.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Now, I need to get back inside, before someone finds out I'm missing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Lydia. I know this can't be easy for you.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I really need to go. Speak to Martin, and good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: No time to talk I'm afraid. Didn't Councillor Andrews want you to speak to Martin Holt about his protest?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Hello again, friend. Isn't it a wonderful day? Sophia Hughes has been dealt with and our wonderfully corrupt Council are out rowing without a paddle. Keep up the good work!

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Good to see you, friend. Excellent work with Sophia Hughes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Martin. I see your protest is growing.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Indeed it is, friend. Would you believe it that apparently some councillor went and summoned a demon in Port Piscarilius? That made a lot of people angry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems the guards have also taken interest.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: No doubt sent by the Council to keep an eye on us. We've got them scared now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe they think things will get violent?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: And yet they're the ones with the weapons. Interesting, huh?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Anyway, what can I do for you, friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something important we need to talk about.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Something important? Is it about the Council?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Well, what is it? If you have news on the Council, we must let the people know at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This one needs to stay between you and me.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The people have a right to know, my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They do. But if what I've discovered is true, we can't let the Council realise we know until we have definitive proof.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I see. This sounds serious. What have you learnt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When I confronted Sophia Hughes, she spoke of things the Council did to a man and his daughter.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: A man and his daughter? Surely this can't mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kharedst IV. The last King of Great Kourend.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I knew it! I knew the Council were involved in his death!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like I said, we have no proof. The dying words of a cultist are not going to be enough to convince many. We need real proof.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: And this is why you came to me? You have a plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The King's daughter, Rose. If we can find her, we might be able to find out what happened.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: But my friend, no one knows where Rose is! Some don't even think she's alive any more. Sadly, most people have just forgotten her completely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you haven't! You must have some idea of where they might have taken her.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I'm afraid not. However, I wonder if Rose herself might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... What?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The Council didn't declare Rose mad right away. It wasn't until a few weeks after her father died that she was moved away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how does that help us?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Rose was well known for being a good writer, even at her young age. She documented everything she did. She even wrote her father's memoirs for him.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: If something happened in those few weeks, maybe she wrote about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I understand now. That does make sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I actually ended up acquiring her father's memoirs myself.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: You did? Interesting. The memoirs were auctioned off recently, along with a lot of other royal possessions.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The auction was a request from the King before he died. He wanted the money to go towards helping the poor of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I actually ended up acquiring her father's memoirs myself. There was an interesting page hidden in the book. It suggested that something bad happened to whoever wrote it.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: And you're only mentioning this now? You had proof that something happened to Rose, and you did nothing with it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it wasn't really proof of anything. It gave no details on what actually happened, and no leads to follow. It wasn't even clear if Rose wrote it.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Even still, something like that might have been worth bringing up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, now you know. I don't see how it helps us, though.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The memoirs were auctioned off recently, along with a lot of other royal possessions. The auction was a request from the King before he died. He wanted the money to go towards helping the poor of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The King's been dead for years. How come the auction only happened recently?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: For that you can thank our Council friends. They blocked it for years. They said Rose's things should not be included, as the original request only covered her father's possessions.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I did wonder if they had more malicious motivations, and now you've confirmed it. They clearly didn't want anyone getting their hands on Rose's writings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were there any other writings, apart from the memoirs?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Yes. Rose's personal diary. It went to the Library Historical Archive. I hear they have it on display there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I need to go to the Archive and get that diary.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The Archive isn't the Library. You can't just borrow things. I doubt they'll let you take it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, I wasn't actually planning on asking.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: A [man/woman] who knows what needs to be done. I respect that. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you to the Archive. Come back once you have the diary and we'll discuss our next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to be doing?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Rose's personal diary is on display in the Library Historical Archive. We need to aquire it. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you to the Archive.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Welcome to the Library Historical Archive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question about Rose.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Rose? The princess?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Ah, poor girl. Her tale is such a tragic one. What would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard her diary was on display here. I was wondering if I could read it.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: You are correct that we have her diary. It's in the display case on the eastern wall. That said, I'm afraid I can't let you read it.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: The diary is here to be preserved. However, we do not feel it appropriate to let people read the private thoughts of a young girl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Good to see you, friend. Any luck with that diary?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Good work. Now, let's see if this diary has anything for us...

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Hmm. Doesn't look to be anything obvious, but that's not suprising. Anything too in your face would have been found by the Council long ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The hidden page from the King's memoirs was hidden inside the book cover.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Let's see if the same trick has been used here.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Let's try this...

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: And what do we have here?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Very interesting. See what you make of this.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt after reading Rose's note

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: We should split up and try to solve the riddle. I'll regroup with you at wherever it leads us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a riddle.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: That it is. It seems to be describing a location. If we find that location, maybe we'll find something of use.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: We should split up. The Council could be watching us, and I don't want to make anything too obvious. Let's see who can solve this thing first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt at the Forthos Ruin

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Keep looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could help, you know.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I'll have you know I am helping. I'm looking here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been looking there for a while now.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I'm being thorough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you say so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt again after finding Rose's second note

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I've found another note.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Well what does it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Out in a land that's frozen to the bone. A village of statues, not made of stone.'

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Another riddle. Well, same again then. Let's split up and see if we can find where it leads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll see you soon.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt again before leaving

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: We should split up and try to solve the next riddle. I'll regroup with you at wherever it leads us.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Well here we are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think this is definitely it?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Forthos was the seat of the Hosidius family before it was destroyed in the War of the Five Houses. 'A seat of power, lost in a war of pride.'

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Anyway, looks like I won this one. What's my prize?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think there is one.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Pah, that's where you're wrong my friend. This could lead us to the ultimate prize. This could lead us to the truth behind the delightful Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really do hate them don't you? You know, Lydia told me you worked for them once.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just interesting. You went from working for them to being their biggest opponent. What changed?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I opened my eyes. When I worked for the Council, I was young and naive. But over time, I came to see the truth.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The tipping point came a couple of years ago. I was helping to manage the treasury at the time. We had a big donation come in from a private businessman. Lovely chap.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: He was ever so noble. Something eating away at his conscience, no doubt. He wanted the money to go towards improved education in Port Piscarilius. He felt it would help with the crime levels there.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: That was of course a big no-go for the Council. Helping the people? No chance! Instead, they used only a small amount for that. They spent the rest on castle renovations.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: They claimed the increase in castle tourism would make more money in the long run. A load of tosh! I knew it was just for them.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I walked out that same day. Soon after, I founded Kourend's Royal Society. We've been a bane for the Council ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not mention this before?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Wasn't really important. I'm sure Lydia didn't tell you everything about herself either. We were together once, you know. But she was ever loyal to the Council.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: In the end she decided I wasn't her type, and went off with that Captain Ginea instead. Of course, that didn't work out either. One day she'll stop blaming her problems on everyone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this relevant?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Nope. And neither is my history with the Council. Now, let's crack on. Rose led us here for a reason. There must be something hidden nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Rose?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: She was born to King Kharedst IV as his first and only child. Her mother died when she was very young, so her father mostly looked after her on his own. The two were very close.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: She was only 11 when her father died. Even at that age, she had already proven that she was smart, capable and caring. She'd have made a good queen, but the Council put a stop to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She was 11 when the King died? How old would that make her now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 31? So wherever she is, she's spent most of her life there. If she's still alive of course.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: You'd hope that even the Council wouldn't go as far as to kill her, but that's probably wishful thinking. They're as evil as they come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens when we find Rose?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Hopefully she can give us the truth about her father's death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, but say the Council are guilty? What happens then?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Oh they're guilty my friend, make no mistake. We just need proof. Once we have it, we can use it to have them removed from power.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: After that, Rose can take her rightful place on the throne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if she's incapable of doing that?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Let's cross that bridge if we get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Kourend's Royal Society.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The throne of Kourend has sat empty for far too long. After all, what is a kingdom without a king? That was the question I asked myself when I created the society.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: You see, I'd grown tired of our wonderful Council and their dishonourable ways. I wanted a return to how things used to be. Turns out I wasn't alone.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Kourend's Royal Society offers a place for likeminded individuals to share and debate their views without fear of being judged.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not just about debate though is it? You're trying to spread your views to others with your protests.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: We know the Council to be corrupt. We feel it only right that all of Kourend know that. The people have a right to the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's interesting that you focus on power for the people but desire a monarchy. Surely a democracy would be more fitting?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Democracy is just a façade, my friend. The Council! They're meant to be a democracy! It's all a load of rubbish. Democracy is just a way of pretending the people have power.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe, but I don't see how you fix that with a king or queen.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Kings and queens are raised from birth to rule. They know how to do it right. Under a king or queen, this kingdom would flourish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't say I'm convinced.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Even with a monarchy, the people still hold the power. Sometimes it just takes them time to realise it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Lathas of Kandarin was raised from birth to rule. I'm not sure he did a great job.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: And what happened to him in the end?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The people had him executed.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Exactly! Even with a monarchy, the people still hold the power. Sometimes it just takes them time to realise it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get searching.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt at the Settlement Ruins
Talking to Martin Holt again after defeating the assassin

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Crikey! Are you alright?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: So now the Council are sending assassins after us. They've got some nerve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't know it was the Council.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Who else would it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The assassin only named you. Surely the Council would want me gone as well if they knew what we were doing.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Yes, a good point. They clearly don't know what we're doing yet. This must be about the protests instead. They wanted to silence me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again, we don't know that.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: It's the only logical explanation. The Council are running scared. They can feel their grip on Kourend loosening and are desperately trying to stop that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, Council or otherwise, the assassin is dealt with. Now we need to get this panel open and get out of here.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Very true. I tried to open the panel before you arrived, but it needs a key. Perhaps it's hidden nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have a look around.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt again after finding Rose's third note

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The panel had another note behind it.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Excellent. What does this one say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Hearty drinks are a sailor's friend. Where one long journey comes to an end.'

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Another one. I wonder how many there are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only one way to find out, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Indeed. Let's split up again. Be careful though. Who knows if the Council will send another assassin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll see you soon.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt again before leaving

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: We should split up and try to solve the next riddle. I'll regroup with you at wherever it leads us. Be careful though. Who knows if the Council will send another assassin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Ah, you made it. Welcome to Kourend's top holiday destination. Here you'll find all sorts of fun things like cold, cold and cold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can say that again. I'm guessing this panel is what we're after.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Looks like it. Problem is, it's locked and...

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Hang on, do you hear that?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Martin Holt. You have been marked for death.

sections.title: Talking to Martin Holt at The Legless Faun

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Now this is more like it! A nice temperature and a good source of beer. Not to mention the fact that the Council wouldn't dare send an assassin somewhere so built up. Even they're not that stupid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rose certainly went all out. I wonder how she managed it?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Apparently Rose and her father would often make riddles for each other. I'm guessing she was quite fond of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even so, I wonder how she set it all up?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Never underestimate a child, my friend. I remember a gang of thieves a few years back that used them heavily. The kids could get places the adults couldn't. No one ever suspected them either.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: They were such a problem that the Council had to get Lord Shayzien to send some soldiers in to deal with them. Lydia was one of them. This was before she joined the Royal Guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: The Council had them all thrown in prison, even the children. Lydia was distraught. Still, it didn't stop her joining the Royal Guard. She was always blind to the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So were you, once. You said that yourself.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: True, but at least I realised in the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, it was Lydia who sent me to talk to you about all of this in the first place.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So maybe she's not as bad as you say.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Maybe. Anyway, we have a clue to find. Time to get searching.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have a look around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Martin Holt.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Tomas Lawry! Backbone of the Kourend Council!

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: How very kind of you.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Of course, it's a shame the Council are totally spineless.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Hmm. What are you doing here Martin?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I'm here for a drink of course. This is a pub, you know. Lovely place to sit back and relax. You should try it some time.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: This would be easier if you told the truth.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I am telling the truth. Is it illegal to go to the pub now?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: For you, I'm afraid it is. Martin Holt, I am arresting you for inciting violence against the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Inciting violence? You must be joking! All I've done is express my valid concerns. There is no law against that!

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: You have encouraged people to take up arms against the rightful rulers of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I have given the people a voice! You cannot silence us all!

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The people can express their concerns through the proper channels. Now, I must ask that you come with us at once.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: And if I refuse?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Come now, Martin. Let's not go there. You know how it ends.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Fine! But the people will not stand for this. I will not be responsible for what happens now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like I'm on my own now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Councillor.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Oh, hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know of this mage?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Very little. It was David... Sorry, Councillor Andrews, who he contacted first. The rest of us didn't meet him until much later.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: He was an expert in old curses. He could see what was wrong with the King. He told Councillor Andrews and offered his help.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: By then, it was already too late. The King had deteriorated too much. There was only one solution.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know if that's true.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: You weren't there. You didn't see what was happening to him. There was no time to consider other options. We did what we had to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you say so. You said he lives in Molch?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: That's right. In the temple, I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A mysterious mage with no name who lives with some lizardmen. He sure sounds like an upstanding citizen with nothing suspicious about him at all.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: You're not funny. Do you think we'd have worked with him if we knew all of that? We didn't find out about the lizardman connection until much later. By then, the deed had already been done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm suprised you'd even care about lizardman. They're pretty tame compared to demon summoning.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Sophia wasn't always like that. Before she joined that cult, she was just like the rest of us. The only difference was that she had other beliefs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zamorakian beliefs aren't particularly welcome in most places.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: So what? People should be entitled to their beliefs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, but I suspect being entitled to summon demons is more up for debate.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Like I said, she wasn't always like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened with Rose?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I know you probably don't believe it, but we always meant for her to take the throne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'd probably have an easier time believing that if she wasn't lying right here instead.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: She overheard us talking about the King. We couldn't let her tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why? If it was really all about her, why would it matter what happened to you?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Because without us, who would have been left to rule? We had to do it for the good of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you? Or have you lied to yourself so much that you now actually believe it?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: She was like a daughter to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you poisoned her?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: And I have taken care of her every day since! Without me, she wouldn't even be alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is what she has now really much of a life at all?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: You don't understand...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, I don't know if I want to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You need to find out who this mage is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry. I'll work it out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rose?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: What the... Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could ask you the same question.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: This place is private! You're not welcome here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well here's the thing: I don't actually care.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I came here looking for someone, and I think this woman here might be that someone. If she is, you're in a whole different world of trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So tell me, who are you?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: My god...

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: You're looking for Rose, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this her?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: It... Yes, this is her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you are?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: I'm Councillor Liam Orson, of the Kourend Council. But please, you have to understand...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'd very much like to understand. Why are you here with Kourend's missing princess? And why is she just lying there like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suggest you explain everything. You should probably do it fast, as well.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I don't know where to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about with her father. How did he die?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: There was something wrong with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You expect me to believe that?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: It's the truth!

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: He was always a fan of antiques. One day he bought some old flask from a stall in Lovakengj. He began drinking from the flask wherever he went, and it started to change him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Change him?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: The flask was cursed. Some dark magic. He stopped being himself. Started saying things. Things he would do to people. To the citizens!

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: We did what we had to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You killed him. You killed him and took his throne for yourselves.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: No... We never wanted the throne. The throne was always meant for Rose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that clearly didn't happen.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: She was never meant to be involved. We were just going to look after things until she was ready. But she found out what we did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you did this to her?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: We didn't want to hurt her, but we couldn't risk her telling anyone. This was the only way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you failed. She got the word out. It took 20 years, but thanks to her, your rule is at an end.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: So, what happens now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not for me to decide. There's someone who needs to see this.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Is it... Is it really her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: And you say she's not aware of... anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't look like it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: What a mess.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: What of the Councillor? Where is he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I locked him in the other room. Didn't want him running off. I'll get him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can come out now.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Councillor.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Commander.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Liam... tell me it isn't true.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I'm sorry Lydia.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I defended you! I defended all of you! And all this time, you've been lying!

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I swear to you, we did it for the people.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Don't give me that!

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Look at her! Look at what you did to her! She was a child, Liam!

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you do to her? Why is she like this?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: We didn't want to kill her. She didn't deserve that. The mage gave us a potion. He said it would stop her revealing the truth.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Mage? What mage?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: There was an Arceuus mage. He was an expert in curses, and he realised what was happening. He gave us the poison for the King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would have been nice for you to mention this earlier! Who was this mage?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: He never told us his name.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: And you trusted him? Are you serious? Some random mage comes along and tells you to poison the King and you just... do it?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Everything the mage said was true. The King was cursed. We could see it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Did it ever cross your mind that maybe this mage was the one that cursed him in the first place? How do you know he wasn't just using you?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Of course it crossed our minds, but we didn't have a choice. The King was deteriorating by the hour.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You could have gone to Lord Arceuus! You could have gone to Lord Shayzien! You could have gone to anyone apart from some random mage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This mage. Have you met him since?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Yes. Rose's condition...

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You mean the condition you gave her?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Her condition has no cure, but it can be kept in check with regular treatment. He meets with me and gives me the potions she needs.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: How do you know it's not the potions that are doing this to her?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I've done my research. The potions are safe, and she can't live without them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this mage?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: We meet outside Molch. I think he lives in the temple there, with the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This mage. We need to find out who he is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go to Molch. If he's there, I'll find him.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Good luck out there. I'll keep an eye on this... I don't even have a word for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Try not to kill him.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'd rather not talk about it. We have work to focus on. There'll be plenty of time to contemplate later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things back at the castle?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Terrible. I take it you heard about Martin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The arrest? I was there.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Such a stupid move. I told Councillor Andrews not to do it, but he listened to Lawry instead. Now the protestors are even angrier.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: At this rate, it's only a matter of time before things get violent. I even had Councillor Unkar moved to the castle for his own safety.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Not that he deserves safety! What a mess this is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be off.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about the key to that door...

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss. I can give you the key, but firssst I need my lassst egg. It'sss at the hatching area in the ssswamp, to the sssouth wessst of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll bring you the egg.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: It isss marked with the sssymbol of our massster. Go fassst human.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question about that door over there.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss? What about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not got any mages hiding behind it have you?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Magesss? You know of the Yo'kal ahk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yo'kal ahk?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: It isss the name we ussse for the mage you ssspeak of. In your ssspeach, he hasss another name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This mage. Where did he come from?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: He hasss been here sssince our massster ssstill walked among usss. He wasss sssealed away before, but they releasssed him so he could guide usss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to see this mage. How do I get through the door?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: It is closssed, but there are keysss. We shhhamansss carry them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well can I have your key?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Perhapsss. But why shhhould I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could just kill you instead?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: And I could shhhout for help. Humansss are not meant to be in thisss temple. Do you think you can sssurvive usss all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. What do you want in return?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: A trade? Yesss, that worksss well.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: The mage you ssspeak of. He sssends us to fight the humansss. He sssays we mussst do it for our massster, but he isss long gone.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: I am done with thisss fighting. I want to ssserve our massster in other waysss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could help you escape?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: I am a shhhaman. I need no help from humansss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, then?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: The hatching area in the ssswamp, to the sssouth wessst of here. My lassst egg isss there. If I go and it ssstays, it will die in thisss falssse war.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I bring you this egg, you'll give me the key?

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss. The egg isss marked with the sssymbol of our massster. Go fassst human.

sections.title: Talking to Kaht B'alam with his egg

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about that door over there...

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Ussse the key I gave you. That will get you inssside. But be cautiousss of the Yo'kal ahk human. He isss very dangerousss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Ye niauh, human. Perhapsss we will meet again.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about the key to that door...

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Yesss. I can give you the key, but firssst I need my lassst egg.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Thank you, human. You have given usss a chance for a new ssstart. Here isss the key.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Be cautiousss of the Yo'kal ahk human. He isss very dangerousss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Kaht B'alam
lines.text: Ye niauh, human. Perhapsss we will meet again.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: ...actually quite surprised. I thought you of all people would have made more of an effort to find me.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Your greatest achievement was my downfall, my lord. The lizardmen blamed me for your disappearance and sealed me away. I was trapped for centuries.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: And yet, here you are.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I managed to convince their shamans I was on their side. They set me free. I have been trying to find you ever since.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Except you were beaten by a treasure hunter.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: With humble respect, my lord, this treasure hunter is not the one to thank.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: It was me. I was the one who worked out how to find you. I planted the seeds that eventually led him to you.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: A proxy? Interesting. I wouldn't have thought you would be one to hide away.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: The world has changed. Working from the shadows has served me well. And now it has reunited us once more.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Yes, the earthquake. That was a considerable amount of dark energy. Something I could not ignore. But to what end?

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: The same end as always, my lord. Quidamortem has been lost to us, but there are other ways to harness the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I see. And tell me, what of this Council that now rules Kourend? I sense your work at play there.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I needed access to the Altar. They were a means to an end.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: You installed some puppet rulers just to access the Altar? My my, Xamphur, you haven't lost your ambition.

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: Never, my lord. I also hoped that access to Kourend would have further uses, but I fear the Council is done.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: From what I hear, you are likely right. No matter, though. Kourend is of limited use to us.

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: My lord?

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: That kingdom was always a means to an end. A disappointing necessity while we were still shackled.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: But now, those shackles are gone. I have discovered a new way to achieve our goals, and this time, none will stand in our way.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Now, we have much time to make up. Unfortunate, but bearable. Soon time will be of little meaning to us.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Ah, but on that note, it seems this conversation is at an end, for we are no longer alone.

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Deal with your guest Xamphur. We will meet again in the south.

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: It will be done, my lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Veos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Hi there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I think I took a wrong turn somewhere. I'll just go now...

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: Go? No I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: You have heard too much. You have seen too much. You won't be going anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah. Well in that case... Xamphur, was it? How about you tell me what on Gielinor is going on here?

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: Really? You think this is the part where I tell you everything?

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: No, no, no. This is the part where you die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh.

lines.speaker: Xamphur
lines.text: Really now? You actually thought you could defeat me?

lines.speaker: First Lizardman
lines.text: What are we sssupposssed to be doing?

lines.speaker: Second Lizardman
lines.text: The Yo'kal ahk wantsss thisss corpssse removing.

lines.speaker: First Lizardman
lines.text: Are you sssure it'sss dead?

lines.speaker: Second Lizardman
lines.text: It looksss dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh...

lines.speaker: First Lizardman
lines.text: It'sss a zombie! Let'sss get out of here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I should have a look around.

sections.title: Searching the table in the lab

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. I should tell Lydia about this.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You're back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am. Where's the Councillor?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Don't worry, I didn't kill him. I was sick of looking at him, so I moved him somewhere safe. He won't be going anywhere.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Did you find the mage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, but he got away. To be honest, he also left me with more questions than answers.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His name is Xamphur. I overheard a conversation between him and someone else. They talked about the Council, among other things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The weird thing is, the person he was talking to... I think it was Veos.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The treasure hunter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. He disappeared after that earthquake, but I saw him with this mage.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The conversation? What was it about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xamphur called Veos, or whoever it was, his master. It sounded like he had been seeking him out for a long time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They talked about the Council and the Dark Altar. I couldn't make much sense of it, but I managed to look around after they left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From what I found, it seems that Xamphur worked with Councillor Andrews to manipulate the other councillors into killing the King.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Councillor Andrews was in on it from the start? That monster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems he always wanted the throne. Xamphur offered it to him in return for access to the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The Dark Altar? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xamphur has been redirecting power from the Altar, but I don't know why. It seems his work is what caused the recent issues in Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The death? I heard about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Makes you wonder what else he's been responsible for.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'm not sure what any of this means, but it can't be good. We need to do something. But first...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Rose. What do we do with her?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I did some research while you were gone. Liam wasn't lying. The poison they gave her irreversibly destroyed her mind. There is no cure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suspect Xamphur won't be making her any more potions any time soon. Maybe someone in Arceuus could help?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Someone else could make the potions, but all they do is prolong her suffering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you saying?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This is no life for anyone. The truth is, Rose died long ago. All that's left now is an empty shell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I think I understand.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'm sorry, Rose.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
