lines.text: Back when I was a girl, there was this Shayzien patrol that would go past our house each morning. Every day I would run out to wave at them as they went by.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: For as long as I can remember, I always wanted to join the army. 'Become a farmer or a chef,' my parents would say to me. 'The army isn't for you.'

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: But I knew what I wanted, and I knew nothing would stop me from getting it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I saw some nasty things while serving the Council. I'd always tell myself that it was necessary.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Whenever I thought about giving up, I'd ask myself who I'd be replaced with. Even if things weren't perfect, I was in the best place to improve them.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It was all a lie though, wasn't it? It's always been a lie.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I've spent most of my life serving a bunch of murderers; turning away from the truth because I was so scared of being wrong.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: To think it was Martin who was right all along!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can still make this right. We can still stop the Council.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We won't be able to do it alone. The Council still holds the power in Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if that's true. The real power is held by the five houses of Kourend. Together, they could replace the Council and take over the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We'd need to get them all on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leave that with me.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Alright, you gather the five leaders. Convince them to meet us at Xeric's Lookout, south east of Shayzien.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It's where I left Councillor Orson. We won't be disturbed there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Consider it done.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It feels wrong, her being out here. She should have had a proper funeral.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not so bad. It's nice and peaceful. She's finally free from the drama of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I guess you're right.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We'd better get going. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You too.

sections.title: Talking to Commander Fullore at Xeric's Lookout

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Councillor Orson is down there. I'm keeping it locked, just in case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Have you spoken with all the leaders yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Keep at it. We'll need all five of them on board. When you speak to them, tell them to meet us here.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Good day friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the Council...

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I will join you at Xeric's Lookout soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Good day friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus. There's something I need your help with.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We should discuss it in private. It involves the Kourend Council. It's also connected to the recent death here.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I see. Then I will come at once. Where shall we meet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xeric's Lookout.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Very well. I will join you there soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you be departing for Xeric's Lookout soon?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I will. But there are a few things I need to prepare first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Hosidius. I need to discuss something with you.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can't talk about it here. I need you to meet me at Xeric's Lookout.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Well you have helped me in the past. That said, I can't just go running off to Xeric's Lookout for no reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's about the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Ah, I see. Then the day has finally come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: There has been discontent with the Council since the day they took control of the kingdom. That was always going to come to a head at some point.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It seems that point has now arrived. I will depart for Xeric's Lookout shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh look, it's the human. Hello human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So about that Council business...

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Yes, yes. Come to Xeric's Lookout. I'll be there as soon as I've finished this sherry.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh look, it's the human. Hello human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lady Lovakengj. I need you to join me at Xeric's Lookout. There's something urgent we need to discuss.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Urgent?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. We can't discuss it here, but it involves the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh. Well you really should speak to Councillor Unkar. He's part of the Council, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not quite what I meant. It's not something to discuss with the Council themselves. It's something I need the house leaders for.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So will you come?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Come where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To Xeric's Lookout. To discuss this Council business.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Ah yes, the Council business at Xeric's Lookout. I will come at once, but first...

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Jorra! Bring me a sherry.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Yes Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you be departing for Xeric's Lookout soon?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Do you want to get off my back? I already said I'll meet you there. Now clear off.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help. It's about the Council.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Anything to do with that demon you summoned on my docks?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That wasn't me! Besides, I'd have thought you'd be happy to see Sophia Hughes dealt with.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: I'd have preferred it be done without demons being involved. Idiots will be idiots, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Come on then, tell me about this latest Council nonsense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can't discuss it here. We need to be somewhere more private.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Oh I'm sorry. Is my secret hideout in the sewers not private enough for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... not really. Meet me at Xeric's Lookout. We can talk more there.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Fine. But if you waste my time you will seriously regret it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Whatever you say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I will depart for Xeric's Lookout soon, citizen. I have a few things I need to do first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something urgent I need your help with.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: What is it, citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It involves the Kourend Council, but we can't really discuss it here. Come alone to Xeric's Lookout.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Alone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I appreciate it's an unusual request, but yes please. All of Kourend could be at stake here.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well I suppose you have been a friend to Shayzien in the past, and this does sound serious.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Very well citizen. I will depart soon.

sections.title: Talking to Commander Fullore after gathering the leaders

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Any luck with the leaders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Keep at it. We need to find a solution that they all agree on.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Leave me alone! I have nothing to say to you.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well you did it. It's been a while since I've seen the leaders of all five houses together. They're downstairs waiting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is the Councillor there as well?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: He is. I told him about Rose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did he take it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: How do you think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'd better keep a close eye on him.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Indeed. Now, let's get down there.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: This is quite the group you've gathered here.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Indeed it is my lord, but I assure you, it's for a very good reason.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Liam.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: My lords and ladies, there is something I must make you aware of.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: It's regarding myself and my fellow councillors. We...

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: I can't...

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Yes you can Liam, and you will.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Don't you lecture me! You have no right! You killed her!

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What on Gielinor are you fools on about? Killed who?

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: They call me a murderer, but they killed Rose! I kept her safe!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. You killed Rose. You killed her 20 years ago. It's time to stop hiding and accept the truth.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Rose? You speak of the King's daughter. What happened?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This coward here killed her, just like he killed her father.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: We did it for the good of the kingdom...

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You did it for yourselves!

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: The King was cursed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The King was cursed so that you had an excuse to kill him. It was never about the people, it was only about the throne.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: That's not true!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have seen proof with my own eyes. If you think overwise, then you have been fooled as well.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Could you idiots please stop arguing and give us an explanation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Councillor Andrews plotted to kill the King with a mage called Xamphur. Xamphur cursed the King so that Councillor Andrews had an argument to get the others on board.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: And then they killed him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Rose as well.

lines.speaker: Councillor Orson
lines.text: Rose was never meant to get hurt! She overheard us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rose was smart, but do you really think she just happened to overhear you? I've no doubt Councillor Andrews arranged for that to happen as well.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: So it's true! I always knew the Council was corrupt, and now we have the proof!

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: So what do we do with this proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's why we gathered you all here. The Council needs to be removed from power. You five can make that happen.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: If the Council are to be removed, they will also have to be replaced.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Who should replace them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could there be another member of the royal family still out there?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I doubt it. The Council's plan worked because Rose was the King's only heir.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: You'd see us go back to a king? The democracy of the Council was false, but democracy is still the answer. Let the people decide!

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Since when did Lady Piscarilius care for the people? Last I checked, your citizens lived in constant squalor.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Don't you lecture me you warmongering fool! I've done more for this kingdom than you'll ever know!

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well if you really do care for the people, surely a monarch is perfect. They're crying out for one right now.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The royalists do have a lot of support at the moment. But who would this monarch be?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I believe I may have an answer to that.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Royal Accord of Twill.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Indeed. This here is the answer to our issue.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Once, in a time of great need, the five houses of Kourend took control of this kingdom. Now it falls to us to do so again. It's time for a new accord.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: You would see us rule together?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: In a way. Together we would all be responsible for Kourend, but a single individual would rule over the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: A new Royal Accord would require all five us to agree on the contents.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Well don't go thinking you're getting me to sign it. I'll have no part in this.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Lady Lovakengj, you've been silent on all of this. What are your thoughts?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Well... I don't really understand. The Council rule Kourend. We can't just take over.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: We have to. There is no other option.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Goodness... I think I need a sherry.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: A break might be beneficial to all of us. We have a lot to consider.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Good idea. Let's reconvene later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that was fun. What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This Royal Accord seems like our best bet. A clear document signed by all of them that determines the future of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For them to all sign it, they all need to agree on the contents. That's not going to be easy.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: No, but there must be a way. They all seem to have some level of trust in you. Maybe you can convince them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can give it a go.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This could quickly get interesting, but you need to do whatever it takes to get them back at that table. If even one of them walks away, we'll end up with a big problem on our hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll go and talk to them all.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Lord Arceuus?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: He intrigues me. I find there's a certain level of arrogance when it comes to anyone from Arceuus. Living for a thousand years will do that, I guess.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: The thing is, he's not like that. He seems to have a lot more humility than the other leaders.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Still, I find it hard to trust anyone who's been around that long. Think about what he must have seen and done over the years. He'll have more secrets than the rest of them combined.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Lady Piscarilius?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: She's lazy and arrogant. She talks like she cares for the people, but one look at Port Piscarilius shows the truth. She only cares for herself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe there's more to her than meets the eye.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You think she really cares?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't rule it out.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Maybe she should start looking after her citizens then. Thoughts and feelings mean nothing when people are suffering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Lord Hosidius?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: He's well-liked by his people, and he does genuinely seem to care for them. The world might be a better place if more leaders were like him.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: That said, we should still be careful. Everyone has two sides, and he knew exactly what he was doing when he brought out the Royal Accord of Twill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Lord Shayzien?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I have a lot of respect for him. He's achieved a lot in his short time as a ruler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Does there need to be a but?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but there generally is one.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: He's a man of war, not peace. You always need to be careful with someone like that. He's also the youngest of all the rulers. Age often brings experience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Often, but not always.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: True.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Lady Lovakengj?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I think she's the most dangerous of all of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: She seems to be nothing more than a slightly senile dwarf with a love for sherry. If that's true, then she's not the kind of person who should be in charge of anything.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: But maybe that's not who she really is. Maybe it's all just an act. If that's the case, then she's managed to fool everyone around her.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Either way, she's one to keep an eye on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The biggest threat is the one you never see coming.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hello there. Have you spoken with the Tasakaal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Please do so soon. Only they have the power to determine who Xamphur is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hello there. I was hoping you'd come and speak with me. There is much we need to discuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the Council?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Not exactly. That revelation is concerning and needs to be addressed. However, I fear we may be facing a much larger issue.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: When we spoke before, you mentioned that all of this had some connection to the death in Arceuus. Tell me, does that have anything to do with the mage you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. Xamphur was his name. I discovered his hideout in the Lizardman Temple below Molch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He was the one responsible for the device that was redirecting power from the Dark Altar, but I'm not totally sure what he was using the power for. That recent earthquake was also involved somehow.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I see. And did this Xamphur look similar to an Arceuus citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He did. How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Xamphur is not a common name, and yet it is one I have heard before. During the Age of Strife, Xeric himself was assisted by a loyal servant of that same name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't think it's the same person do you?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Once, we would never have considered it. However, a recent discovery you made yourself means it's a very real possibility.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The altar at Quidamortem.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Yes. Thanks to that altar, Xeric and his followers had access to the same power that keeps every citizen of Arceuus alive.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: With that power, they may well have lived much longer than originally believed. It's possible one of them even lives to this day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something else as well. When I found Xamphur, he wasn't alone. I think the other person was Veos. The weird thing is, Xamphur spoke as if Veos was his master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't exactly the first odd occurrence when it comes to Veos either. A while back I helped him with some strange client of his.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When we were done, this client spoke through Veos himself. The things he said were... strange.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hmm. This is interesting and concerning. Perhaps this client is the one Xamphur considers his master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if Veos' client is Xamphur's master, and if Xamphur once served Xeric, could that mean...

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Be careful, friend. Empty speculation will be of little help to us here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But surely it's an option we should consider?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Of course. However, if you consider one option too closely, you'll become blind to all other possibilities. What we need here is proof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do we get that?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: My friend, I must ask that you once again to pay a visit to the Tasakaal at Mount Karuulm. With their power, they will be able to determine if this Xamphur is the same one that served Xeric.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: The power of the Dark Altar leaves traces. If Xamphur's soul inhabits a body created using the Dark Altar, the Tasakaal will be able to determine when that body was formed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would they not need his body for that?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Ideally, but I suspect this will also work.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Since you destroyed it, we've been researching this device to try and determine the origin. Thanks to you, we now know that it was made by Xamphur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll take the device to the Tasakaal and see what they can do with it. However, I also need to ask for your support with this new Royal Accord.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: The Council must be removed and a replacement is needed. I will gladly support the new Royal Accord. But first, we must focus on Xamphur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll be back once I've discovered something from the Tasakaal.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that barbarian camp?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Very well. Let me know if you discover anything. The camp is directly south of here in the Kourend Woodland.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there. I'm guessing you want to discuss the Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. I assume you won't have any issues supporting it, considering it was your idea?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It's a messy business, but the Council has to go. That means a replacement is needed. A new Royal Accord is our best hope for a peaceful transition of power.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Now, that said, there are some preparations I need to make before we finalise things. Perhaps you could help me with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if it gets things done faster, then I guess so.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: As with the Royal Accord of Twill, our new accord will see power transferred to the five houses. However, only one of them will rule.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: The Royal Accord of Twill saw each of the houses take it in turns to do this, but we may want to take a different approach this time.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I'm almost certain that Lord Arceuus and Lady Piscarilius will not wish to rule. Lady Lovakengj likely won't either. That leaves myself and Lord Shayzien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And would you be willing to take on that role?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Well, that's where my preparations come in. While I can't say I'm too keen on ruling Kourend, I'm not sure if Lord Shayzien is the right man for the job either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Take a look through the pages of Kourend's history and you'll soon see that soldiers do not make for good rulers.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: As such, before any final decision is made, there's something I'd like to look into when it comes to Lord Shayzien.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: As you probably won't be too surprised to hear, I have people who keep a close eye on things around Kourend. They let me know of any interesting occurrences that could impact Hosidius.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Not too long ago, I was informed that Lord Shayzien went for a private walk in the Kourend Woodland. While there, he stopped off at a small Barbarian Camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's an interesting place for him to visit.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It is. I'm sure it's nothing to worry about, but I'd like to be sure before we make any big decisions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I can go and take a look around this camp.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: That would be appreciated. It's directly south of here in the Kourend Woodland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll let you know if I find anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, it's the human. Hello human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into things with Councillor Unkar. I'll let you know once I find something.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Okay human. I'm sure you'll soon see that Councillor Unkar is innocent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, it's the human. Hello human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to talk to you about this Royal Accord. Would you be willing to support it?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Royal Accord? Hmm...

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: No I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Why not what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why can't you support the new Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Ah yes, the new Royal Accord. Well Councillor Unkar has been a good dwarf his whole life. He can't be involved in any of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know it's hard to hear, but I think he is.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: No, no, no. That's not right at all. You must be getting confused somewhere. Councillor Andrews is the problem.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, I know! We can replace Councillor Andrews with Councillor Unkar. What an excellent idea! This calls for a sherry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lady Lovakengj please, you have to believe me! Councillor Unkar isn't innocent in this.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Well you can't go believing Councillor Orson! He's clearly just jealous of Councillor Unkar's success.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well how about I find some proof that Councillor Unkar is involved? Then will you support the Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh... well I suppose so, but I don't think you'll find any. Councillor Unkar is innocent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Let me chat to Commander Fullore about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Have you spoken to Mori yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: From what I hear, she spends most of her time in the Arceuus Church. Now clear off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to talk about this Royal Accord.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Don't waste your time. I will never sign that thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'd see the corrupt Council stay in charge?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Of course not, you fool. Did you not listen earlier? The people should decide, not us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it seems like there's a clear solution to this then.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: I doubt that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For the Royal Accord to be finalised, we can have the people vote on it. That way, they get the final say.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: It would still see us return to a single ruler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only if that's what people want. If they vote no, we come up with an alternative arrangement and let them vote on that instead.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Eugh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come on now. If you do care for the people, this is the best option.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Fine. It's still a stupid move, but if there's to be a vote, I might be willing to support it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Yes. First, there's something else we need to discuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course there is.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: While you've been running around chasing dead princesses and mad mages, you've forgotten what started all of this. You summoned a demon in my home!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... I killed a demon that someone else summoned in your home. You should thank me.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Really? Thank you for being as useless as I feared.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about we skip over the insults this time and you just tell me what you want.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: The Disciples of Yama are a big problem. They need dealing with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell Lord Shayzien to send the army in, then. They literally live right on his doorstep.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Exactly! They live on his doorstep and he cares more about stupid lizardmen. The man is clearly as incompetent as you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. So it sounds like you want me to take on this cult singlehandedly? I feel like that won't go well.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Of course I don't expect you to take on an entire cult! What you need to do is cut the head off the snake. Remove the influence of their leader and they won't be a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what? Kill Yama?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: No. Yama has no presence among his followers. At least, not yet. If you remove their connection to him, he won't be able to communicate with them.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: I know of a mage in Arceuus who's hopefully not as useless as you or Lord Shayzien. Her name is Mori and she's been investigating the Disciples of Yama. She should be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woah! Slow down there. How do you know all of this?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Because I did some digging. Oddly enough I wasn't going to ignore a corrupt councillor summoning a demon on my docks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You learnt all of this just with a bit of digging? There's something you're not telling me here. Besides, why would you care so much about this compared to everything else going on?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: This conversation is pointless. If you want my support, this is the price. Now go and talk to Mori. I hear she spends most of her time in the Arceuus Church.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Hello there citizen. Have you found that journal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: It's critical that you do so, as soon as possible. It will likely be in Lord Hosidius' hidden office below the Hosidius Vinery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll let you know what I find.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Ah, there you are citizen. There's something I need to discuss with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Correct, citizen. Once the Council are removed from power, we will need a new ruler. I for one think this Royal Accord is the perfect move.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I think we can also agree that when the times comes, I will be a suitable candidate to rule. These are unstable times. My military leadership will put me in a good position to take over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think the other leaders will support that?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I've no doubt they'll consider me a strong contender. That said, I expect Lord Hosidius will also be considered. On that note, there's a job I have that would be perfect for one with your skills.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Before we come to finalise this Royal Accord, we need to be sure that everyone is in a place where they can pick the right ruler.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: While Lord Hosidius seems like a good man for the job, I've recently obtained some intelligence that could suggest otherwise. I'd like you to look into this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Hosidius? Interesting... What is this intelligence?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Some of my best people have been looking into the disappearance of Phileas Rimor and King Shayzien VII's journal. We now believe we have located both.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... Phileas Rimor?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: That's right. Is there a problem, citizen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... no. Sorry, carry on.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Very well... I will personally see to the rescue of Phileas. I need you to recover the journal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... I thought this was about Lord Hosidius?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: That it is, citizen. If our intelligence is to be believed, Lord Hosidius is now in possession of the journal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't make sense! Why would Lord Hosidius want that journal?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: That I don't know, citizen. Lord Hosidius is meant to be an ally to Shayzien. After all, he's married to my sister.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Perhaps this is some plot of his to grow his influence. He may wish to use the journal to discredit my family. Regardless, I need you to retrieve it. Only then will we be able to determine the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is all very odd. Do you know where he keeps the journal?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Apparently he has a private office hidden below the Hosidius Vinery. That will likely be where he's keeping it. You'll need to go there, find out how to enter this office and then recover the journal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll let you know what I find.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Take a defence potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Add some volcanic sulphur.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Then return to us.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And we will enchant it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And needs our help again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know that?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: It is our place to know.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Come human.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Tell us what you desire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could take a look at this device and tell me more about the person that made it.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why what?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Why should we help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because this device is the thing that was redirecting power from the Dark Altar. The person who made it was also responsible for the trapped soul that needed freeing.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: The meddling mortal.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: You have our attention.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And so we shall help you.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Hold still.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: And we will begin.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Many have touched this device.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Humans.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And two others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Two others?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: One touched by fire.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: And one touched by darkness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Darkness? That sounds like Xamphur. Can you tell how old he is?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: His body was born long ago.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: In the time of the great betrayer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great betrayer? Do you mean Xeric?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Do not speak that name.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: It has no place here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know of him?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: We know all too well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But is that who you speak of?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Lord Arceuus was right. What of this other person, the one touched by fire?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: They are one of many.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Once born of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Now a child of fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Pyromancer? Xamphur is working with a Pyromancer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... I recently helped to repair some damage to the Doors of Dinh. What if this Pyromancer was the one responsible?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Impossible to say.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: But a coincidence is unlikely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then what's to stop them doing it again? We have to do something!

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: We?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: We have no part in this.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: We have already helped you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Keeping the balance between life and death. That's your thing isn't it? If the Wintertodt escapes, it will destroy all life in Kourend. That doesn't seem very balanced!

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: The human makes a valid point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it's been known to happen.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The Dark Altar powers the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: We understand this power.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: We can help reinforce them.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: To prevent further sabotage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: Take a defence potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Add some volcanic sulphur.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Then return to us.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And we will enchant it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get volcanic sulphur?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: It can be mined in Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got it. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sulphur in a defence potion. Got it. I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Speaking to the Tasaskaal after making the potion

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Take your potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Pour it on the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And your work will be done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll get it sorted.

sections.title: Speaking to the Tasaskaal if the potion is lost

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: You are lucky.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: We prepared for such an outcome.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Now take the potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Pour it on the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And your work will be done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll get it sorted.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: The human returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Then hold still.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: And we will begin.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: It is done.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Ket-Jor
lines.text: Now take the potion.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Mej-San
lines.text: Pour it on the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Kaal-Xil-Dar
lines.text: And your work will be done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll get it sorted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's that done. I'd better get back to Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Hey! What are you doing with those doors?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing...

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Well you can either go through them or leave them alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll leave them alone...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hello there. You have my support with the Royal Accord. You should speak with the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hello there. Have you spoken with the Tasakaal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. They confirmed that this Xamphur is the same one that served Xeric.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: So it's as I feared. This is not good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's more. We discovered that Xamphur has been working with a Pyromancer. That means he may have been involved in the sabotage of the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: A Pyromancer? Do you know who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Are you sure about this? Had the Doors of Dinh fully failed, the Wintertodt would have destroyed Kourend. What would Xamphur gain from that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, but the Tasakaal themselves confirmed it.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I will have someone look into this. We'll also use this new knowledge to try and locate Xamphur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His master said they'd meet again in the south.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Interesting. This is useful information that could help our search. I wonder... could they mean the southern Kingdom of Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Ah, but that's one for another time. I believe we've learnt all we can about Xamphur for now. Until we can locate him, we must turn our focus towards the Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll support the new Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I will. While I have no desire to rule myself, I'll gladly support one of my fellow leaders in that role.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'd better go and speak with the others.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Adventurer! You came!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phileas?

lines.speaker: Barbarian Warlord
lines.text: You shouldn't be here! Time to die!

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Is it safe to free me yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I need to go and speak to Lord Hosidius. Don't worry, you'll be safe soon.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Alright. But please, don't take long.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: You came for me! I knew you would!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand Phileas. Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: These barbarians raided my home! They took me prisoner. I thought they were going to kill me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: I don't know. I heard one of them saying they'd been paid to do it. But who would pay to have me captured?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! What about that parcel? The one with King Shayzien VII's journal. Do you have it?

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: No. They took it from me. You don't think that's what this was about, do you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure, but I don't like this at all. I need to speak with Lord Hosidius.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Lord Hosidius? What's he got to do with this? And what about me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's too much to explain right now. Now, there are a lot of barbarians outside, so I can't free you at the moment. I'll go get help. Don't worry, you'll be safe soon.

lines.speaker: Phileas Rimor
lines.text: Alright. But please, don't take long.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there. Thank you for your help earlier. You'll be pleased to know that Phileas is now safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to hear. Thank you for taking care of him. I'd better go and speak with the others.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that barbarian camp?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to get some people down there urgently! The barbarians have Phileas Rimor prisoner.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Phileas Rimor? Who's Phileas Rimor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a retired soldier from Shayzien. I helped him out with something a while ago, but he went missing. Turns out these barbarians were paid to take him.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Paid by whom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know for sure, but I doubt Lord Shayzien's visit was a coincidence.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: So, it's as I feared. Still, that doesn't explain why Lord Shayzien would pay for one of his own citizens to be captured.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The thing I was helping Phileas with involved a mysterious parcel he'd received. The parcel contained King Shayzien VII's journal. When Phileas was captured, it was taken by the barbarians.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before that happened, I followed some clues in the journal. They eventually led me to the discovery that King Shayzien VII may have been responsible for the creation of the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Ah, the discovery at Quidamortem. This is all starting to make sense. It's long been believed that King Shayzien VII eventually became Xeric. That discovery did little to suggest otherwise.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Lord Shayzien wouldn't have been too happy of further evidence that Xeric was part of his family. He must have made arrangements to hide as much of that evidence as possible.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Thank you, friend. I think we have all the information we need. I'll have some of my people head to this camp. They'll ensure Phileas is safe.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It's probably best he disappear for a while, for his own safety. I'll make the arrangements.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What of Lord Shayzien?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: For now, nothing. We should keep this information to ourselves, until the time is right. As for the Royal Accord, I am ready to finalise things when you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I guess I'd better go and speak with the others.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Any luck with the leaders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Keep at it. We need to find a solution that they all agree on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help with Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Have you spoken to Martin? He's in the Shayzien Prison, just west of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and see what he knows.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Any luck with the leaders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Keep at it. We need to find a solution that they all agree on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help with Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She doesn't believe that Councillor Unkar has anything to do with this. She wants proof he's involved before she signs the new Royal Accord.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: That's not too suprising. The two of them are old friends. I've got nothing on him, but I can think of one person who might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Exactly. His group has no doubt kept tabs on all of the councillors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he's been arrested. I can't just go and break him out.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: No, but you can still talk to him. You've helped the Council enough that no one would think much of it if you paid him a visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair point, I suppose. If anyone asks, I can claim I'm trying to round up more of his group.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Nice idea. They took him to the Shayzien Prison, just west of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll go and see what he knows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something about the leaders.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Martin. Remind me, where is this meeting?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: In the Shayzien Graveyard of Heroes. Look for a dwarf. He'll speak with you if you say the passphrase: 'Evil only triumphs when the good do nothing.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was it. Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Not a problem. Now get out there and finish this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Martin!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sorry, but I can't let you talk to the prisoners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm here on Council business. I need to learn more about his group.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Ah yes! You're the one that helped with Councillor Hughes. Carry on then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things Martin?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Oh wonderful! There's free food and a bed! Can you believe it? Luxury compared to anything in Port Piscarilius!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm glad you're keeping your spirits up. Now, I need your help with something.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Of course. Right down to business. As you can see, I'm a little indisposed right now. Apparently the right to protest isn't so much of a right after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, it's just information I need. Things have progressed with the Council. I can't say any more here, but we're close. However, I need information on Councillor Unkar.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Excellent work my friend! From the moment I met you, I knew you were something special. Now, what information do you need on him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything that could prove his corruption.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: If I had information on his corruption, I might not be stood in a cell right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, but you must have something on him. Anything could be helpful at this point.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: I see. Well, there's a member of the Royal Society who has close access to Councillor Unkar's dealings. He gives me regular reports, but there hasn't been anything of note yet.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Still, things have progressed fast recently. Maybe he's had more luck since my imprisonment. As I'm stuck in here, perhaps you should go on my behalf to our usual meeting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan. Where does this meeting take place?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: In the Shayzien Graveyard of Heroes. Look for a dwarf. He'll speak with you if you say the passphrase: 'Evil only triumphs when the good do nothing.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the help, Martin. We'll have you out of there soon.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Good luck friend. Finish this for all of us!

sections.title: Talking to Jorra again after defeating the assassin

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: That was close! Are you okay human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Martin warned me this would happen. The Council are trying to silence us!

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: It's not safe for me here. I need to get back to Lovakengj. Here, this should hopefully give you what you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Jorra. Stay safe.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: You have what you need human. Now I need to get back to Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know you! You're Jorra, Lady Lovakengj's assistant.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: That's right. What brings you here, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The evening owl doesn't eat much pudding.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Maybe you should go and lie down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Evil only triumphs when the good do nothing.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Never have truer words been spoken. Martin sent you, right? I wasn't sure if anyone would come after he got arrested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. So you're with the Royal Society? How come?

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Why wouldn't I be? The Council are nothing but trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But things don't seem too bad in Lovakengj with them in charge.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Lady Lovakengj is in charge of Lovakengj, not the Council. Sure, Councillor Unkar might act like he plays a big part, but it's all empty words.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you not get sick of her asking for sherry?

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Not really. It's not like it's a hard job. Councillor Unkar would see us all work to death in the mines instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well Councillor Unkar is why I'm here. I'm hoping you can give me something of note on him.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Any other day you'd be wasting your time, but today you're in luck. Due to the protests, Councillor Unkar has been staying at the castle for safety. I've had a chance to go through his private documents.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: I think you'll find...

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Wait! What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no. Not again!

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Jorra Ashtar. You have been marked for death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If tomorrow never comes, what is today?

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Maybe you should go and lie down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Jorra
lines.text: Huh... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, it's the human. Hello human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh... Let's see now...

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Do you have any sherry?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sherry? No.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh... Well I'm fine then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll go speak with the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, it's the human. Hello human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a declaration from the Council that I think you should see.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: What? But... I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: The Council wanted to remove me from power? They can't do that! It's not their right!

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: And Councillor Unkar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry. Do you now see why we need to stop them?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Yes... I understand. I will support this Royal Accord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And do you wish to be considered for the throne?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh... No. Leave that for someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. Thank you Lady Lovakengj. I'd better go and speak with the others.

sections.title: Talking to Mori again without the required items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Mori.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Hello again. Do you have some molten glass and a dark essence block yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll come back once I have them.

sections.title: Talking to Mori again with the required items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Mori.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Hello again. Do you have some molten glass and a dark essence block yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have them right here.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Right. You'll need to hide this somewhere in the Chasm of Fire. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Thanks Mori.

sections.title: Talking to Mori again after obtaining the nullifier

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Mori. What should I be doing with this device?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: You just need to hide it somewhere in the Chasm of Fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Thanks Mori.

sections.title: Talking to Mori again after losing the nullifier

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Mori. Do you think you could make me another one of those devices?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: I have enough supplies for another. Here, you'll need to hide it somewhere in the Chasm of Fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Thanks Mori.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Mori.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Hello again. Thank you for everything you did for Thana. You are a good friend to the people of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem. Anyway, there was something I was hoping you could help me with.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Of course. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a situation with the Disciples of Yama. I hear you've been looking into them.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Well... Yes, I have, but that research was meant to be a secret...

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Ah well, you're trustworthy I suppose. What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know of their leader, Yama?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: He's a bit of a mystery. He's a demon of some sorts, but he doesn't seem to have any actual presence in Kourend yet. In fact, until recently, he had no connection to the cult.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Disciples of Yama once had no connection to Yama?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: It does sound odd, but back then they were known as the Disciples of Chaos. They were just a small Zamorakian cult of little note.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. So if Yama has no presence among his followers, how does he communicate with them?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Through another demon called the Voice of Yama.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I killed this Voice of Yama, the cultists would lose their connection to Yama?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: For a time, though I imagine the Voice of Yama would be quickly replaced. However, if you wanted to remove that connection, I do have another idea...

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: After what happened with Thana, I volunteered to help out with the Dark Altar investigations. The device used there was redirecting power from the Altar, but a similar device could block a magical connection entirely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see. So we could use a device like that to block the connection between the Voice of Yama and Yama himself?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: That's right. We never tried it before because the Dark Altar issues were more pressing, but I don't see why it wouldn't work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. So where can I get one of these devices?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: I should be able to make you one. I'll need some molten glass and a dark essence block.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have those right here.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Huh... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: Right. You'll need to hide this somewhere in the Chasm of Fire. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Thanks Mori.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a dark essence block?

lines.speaker: Mori
lines.text: You can mine dense essence blocks from the mine north east of here. They can be turned into dark essence blocks using the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll come back once I have what you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can get those for you. I'll come back once I have them.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Yama
lines.text: Ah, there you are. Our master foresaw your arrival.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... What?

lines.speaker: Disciple of Yama
lines.text: And you have the device as well. How wonderful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you...

lines.speaker: Disciple of Yama
lines.text: Oh look at you. You need a good rest. Here, have a lie down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's the device hidden. Those Disciples of Yama won't be a problem anymore. I'd better tell Lady Piscarilius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Clear off then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I need to speak with the others anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Have you dealt with the Disciples of Yama yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all done.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely. They won't be a problem anymore.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So will you now support this Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Yes, but it's still a stupid idea. And don't even think about trying to put me in charge or something. I'll have no more part in this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think you need to worry about that. Now, I'd better go and speak with the others.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Good. I've had enough of talking with you.

lines.speaker: Pandur Hosidius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear your father has an office here.

lines.speaker: Pandur Hosidius
lines.text: Office? What office?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A secret one, I'm guessing?

lines.speaker: Pandur Hosidius
lines.text: There can't be any secret offices here. I'd have noticed. I notice lots of things, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well in that case, have you noticed your father doing anything interesting here in the past?

lines.speaker: Pandur Hosidius
lines.text: He sometimes comes to inspect the wine barrels. I like to inspect them myself sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More than inspect, by the looks of things. Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Pandur Hosidius
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Hello there, citizen. Thank you for your help earlier. I am ready to support this Royal Accord!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll go and talk with Commander Fullore.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Hello there, citizen. Have you found that journal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Excellent work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's sealed shut somehow.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Not to worry, I should be able to take care of that. When the time comes to finalise the Royal Accord, I will have the information we need to decide upon a new ruler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Thank you, Lord Shayzien. I should speak to Commander Fullore.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Come with me. There's something you need to see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Just come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the... Is he...?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Yup.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened? Did he jump?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I've no idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't think he was pushed, do you?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: What can we do? Even Lord Arceuus can't bring back the dead. He's gone, and that's that.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Why did any of this have to happen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can't undo what's been done, but we can still put an end to all this.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Does that mean you have good news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kind of. I think I've managed to convince all the leaders to sign the Accord. That said, I have no idea who they'll pick to rule.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well, we'll just have to make it work somehow. Maybe then we can finally end this nightmare.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I'll deal with this mess. Once I'm done, let's gather everyone downstairs again.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Shall we get on with this then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Where's Lord Hosidius and Lord Shayzien?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Lord Hosidius requested a private word with Lord Shayzien outside. I'm sure they'll be here soon.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: In fact, I think I hear them now.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Now that everyone is here, I think we can begin.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: What of the Councillor? Is he not joining us?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Councillor Orson is dead.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Dead? What happened?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Jumped from the top of the tower, probably. It doesn't matter, the world won't miss him. Let's move on.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Not the attitude I'd take, but it's true that we can't dwell on this. We need to focus on stopping Councillor Andrews.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Between the five of us, we can remove the Council from power with little violence. But what then? Are we willing to support a new Royal Accord?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Lord Arceuus, let's start with you. What will it take for a new Royal Accord to gain your support?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: My position is a unique one. As such, I feel it would be inappropriate for me to involve myself too heavily in this. That said, I will gladly support the decision made by this group.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You would not wish to rule yourself?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I will gladly advise the next ruler in any way I can, but it should not be me who takes the throne. I would recommend you look to Lord Hosidius or Lord Shayzien for that.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Very well. Lady Piscarilius?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: For the record, I think this is all a bad idea. Still, you're all clearly set on it, so I won't try and stop you.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: However, I stand by what I said before. The people should have a say. Anything we decide here should not be finalised until the people have voted on it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Any thoughts from anyone?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I would be happy to see the people have a say.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It could be a risky move.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: And? Without it, I'm not signing your precious document. If I don't sign, you get nothing.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hmm. Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Assuming the people agree to the new Accord, who do you think should rule the kingdom?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Well not me, that's for sure. I can't imagine anything worse. Get one of these fools to do it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Lady Lovakengj?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Huh? Me?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Yes. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Well... This is all quite something. I suppose the Council have to go though, don't they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you be willing to take over?

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh... No, I don't think so. I think I'll have a sherry instead.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Lord Shayzien. What are your thoughts?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well... I think the Royal Accord of Twill is a good starting point, but we'll need to make some changes for it to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Lady Piscarilius wants the support of the people. Right now, they cry for a king. That means whoever takes the throne should take on the title of king or queen.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I also feel the act of transferring rulership from one house to the next - as in the original Accord - isn't going to work for us. What we need is consistency.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Would you be open to taking on the role yourself?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Err... No, I don't think that would be wise.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Are you sure? You seem to be a perfect candidate.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I don't feel I would be best placed for it. It would be better if I kept my focus on the important task of protecting Kourend.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I suggest we make Lord Hosidius the new ruler of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Lord Hosidius?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Thank you, Lord Shayzien. Taking the throne is not a duty to be considered lightly, but I believe my family and I have what it takes to shoulder this responsibility.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This decision will forever change the future of Kourend. If we go forward with this, the Hosidius family will be the new royal family of Kourend.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Well... This isn't quite what I expected, but sure. Let the idiot have his throne. As long as the people agree.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: He can do it, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Lord Hosidius would be a fitting choice for the role, assuming no one else here is willing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure about this Lord Shayzien?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: I... I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then it sounds like we have an agreement.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: My friends, thank you for putting your trust in me. This kingdom has suffered through much of late, but now it is time for that suffering to end.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: In honour of her sacrifice, I name this declaration Rose's Accord. With it, the Kingdom of Great Kourend will no longer be a kingdom divided.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I promise not to let you down.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: So... What just happened there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems we might have a new king.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: But Lord Shayzien? He seemed like a perfect candidate, but he didn't even want to be considered. What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I might have some idea, but I'm still a bit confused to be honest.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well, Lord Hosidius wants to have a word with you. Perhaps he can shed some light on all of this. He's downstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and speak to him.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: We will have to talk more later my friend. For now, the Council needs to be dealt with. You should speak with Commander Fullore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Hello there. No doubt you have questions about what just occurred.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can say that again!

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I'm sorry for keeping you in the dark, my friend. It wasn't anything against you. I just couldn't afford to take any risks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You planned this all along, didn't you? That's why you kept the Royal Accord of Twill.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It was inevitable that one day the Council would fall. Their rule has always been fragile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you made sure that you'd be the one to replace them.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It gives me no pleasure, but this was needed for the safety of all of Kourend. Apart from Lord Shayzien, the other rulers had no interest in the throne. It was always going to be him or me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you didn't want it to be him?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It was for the safety of Kourend. Lord Shayzien has a good military mind, but he is no ruler. After all, you saw what he did with Phileas.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: To Lord Shayzien, family name is everything. Above all else, he craves glory for himself and his house. Under him, war would have been inevitable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: War with who?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
