lines.text: Varlamore, the kingdom to the south. Our two kingdoms were in conflict less than a hundred years ago, and relations remain sour. It would take very little for that conflict to be reignited.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really think Lord Shayzien would have declared war?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I can't say for sure, but it was a risk I did not intend to take.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So how did you do it?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: A while ago, I managed to acquire the old journal of King Shayzien VII. I knew that I could use it to discredit Lord Shayzien and his family, so I made some arrangements.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phileas' mystery parcel! That was you? You acted like you didn't even know him earlier!

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Apologies for the misdirection. It was indeed me who sent that parcel.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Had I revealed the contents of the journal myself, I would likely have just been dismissed as trying to create a rift between our two houses. Phileas was the perfect third party.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But then Lord Shayzien had Phileas captured before he could reveal too much. This is all starting to make sense. You know, he actually blamed you for that!

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Phileas' disappearance was an interesting twist. I hadn't expected something like that. Still, it actually made things even easier in the end. Once Phileas was safe, I told Lord Shayzien and gave him a choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I see. You let him pick between supporting you or being exposed.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Exactly. The truth about Phileas would have lost him the support of his people and ruined his reputation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So in the end, you never actually needed to discredit him at all. What about the journal? Lord Shayzien sent me to steal it from you, but surely he already had it.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I didn't want to risk losing the real journal. The one I sent to Phileas was a copy, with many bits removed. While I covered my tracks well, Lord Shayzien must have worked out I was involved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And he sent me to recover the real journal.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Yes. Unfortunately for him, the one you stole was a fake. The real one is somewhere much safer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really thought of everything didn't you? How about the Accord? That was essential to your plans. I'm guessing it was no accident that Artur found it.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I can't take too much credit from Artur there. He did do most of the work himself. I'd been looking into the Accord for a while when Artur found my research.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: When he went missing, I feared the worst. It was a nice surprise when you not only brought him back, but the Accord as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is a lot to take in. I don't know if I like any of it.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Nor do I like it myself. However, when it comes to politics, there are no perfect options. Instead, all we can do is pick the best option we have.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Anyway, we will have plenty of time to discuss this later. For now, the Council needs to be dealt with. You should speak with Commander Fullore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hello there. It's time for us to deal with the Council. You should speak with Commander Fullore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. I just need to talk to Lord Hosidius first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Hello there. It's time for us to deal with the Council. You should speak with Commander Fullore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Shayzien.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Ah... Hello there citizen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened there? Why didn't you put yourself forward to become king?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Well... It was a complex choice, citizen. I wouldn't worry about it. We're all ready to deal with the Council. Let's focus on that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, it's the human. Shouldn't you be speaking with Commander Fullore?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I need to go talk to Lord Hosidius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lady Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Lady Vulcana Lovakengj
lines.text: Oh, it's the human. Shouldn't you be speaking with Commander Fullore?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Clear off then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I need to speak with Lord Hosidius anyway.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Clear off then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I need to speak with Commander Fullore anyway.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Lord Hosidius wants to have a word with you. He's downstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and speak to him.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: How did your chat with Lord Hosidius go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was definitely illuminating. It seems there was only ever really one option for Kourend's new ruler.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We'll have to talk about that later. It's time to pay a visit to the Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we doing this?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We'll join the five leaders and head to the castle. There, we'll tell the councillors everything we know and present Rose's Accord to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think they'll come quietly?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: They won't have a choice. Lord Shayzien commands the Shayzien Army, and I command the Royal Guard. They'll have no one to protect them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens then?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: They'll be imprisoned. It will be for the new ruler to decide their fates. As for that, we'll aim to hold the vote Lady Piscarilius requested as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Assuming the people support Rose's Accord, we can have Lord Hosidius crowned within a few days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think they'll support it?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I think so. Many are already calling out for a king, and Lord Hosidius is popular across the whole kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like we have everything covered, then. When do we depart?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: As soon as you're ready. From what I hear, the protest is getting close to breaking point. We can't waste any more time.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Ready to pay the Council a visit?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do this.

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: Hold the line! Under no circumstances can this castle be breached!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: But Captain... They're citizens...

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: Did you not hear me? Hold the line!

lines.speaker: Protester
lines.text: That's right, hold the line! Show everyone who you really serve!

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: We serve Great Kourend! You are all in violation of the laws of this kingdom!

lines.speaker: Protester
lines.text: Laws set by your false Council! If you really served Kourend, you wouldn't be here defending those monsters!

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: Step back citizen!

lines.speaker: Protester
lines.text: Or what? You'll kill me? Go on! I dare you to do it! See what happens!

lines.speaker: Protester
lines.text: Yeah! You can't kill us all!

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: Stand back! I'm warning you!

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Stand down, Captain Rachelle. This situation is now under my command.

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: Lord Shayzien? What's going on?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: These citizens are to be protected. Ensure they do not enter the castle, but do not allow any harm to come to them.

lines.speaker: Captain Rachelle
lines.text: As you wish, Lord Shayzien. It will be done.

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Thank you, Captain. Now, myself and the other leaders will go and speak with the councillors. This situation will be resolved soon.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Ah, Lord Shayzien. It's good to have you here. Hopefully with your support we can get this protest under control.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Though I must say, I'm a bit confused as to why everyone else is here. Is something happening?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: That would be one way to describe it Councillor. Lord Hosidius.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Councillor Andrews, Councillor Unkar. We are here to present a joint declaration from the five houses of Great Kourend.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Declaration? I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: We, the leaders of the five houses of Great Kourend, declare that the Kourend Council is no longer fit to rule. As such, you are relieved of your positions with immediate effect.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: What the... What is this?

lines.speaker: Lord Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Furthermore, you will both hand yourselves over to Lord Shayzien's soldiers. They will take you into custody where you will await your trial.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Trial? Have you gone mad? Trial for what?

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: For the crimes of murder and high treason. Along with the now-deceased Liam Orson and Sophia Hughes, the two of you successfully conspired to murder King Kharedst IV and Crown Princess Rose.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Liam's... dead?

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: This is insanity! Guards! Arrest them at once!

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Soldiers of the Kourend Royal Guard, you serve the rightful ruler of Kourend! That is not this man. Stand down and step aside!

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: No! You can't do this!

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: David... It's over...

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Over? Only I decide when it's over! I am the rightful ruler of this kingdom and you all answer to me!

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Guards.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: Stay away from me! This is my throne! I worked too hard for you to just take it away! I'd rather die!

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Do it.

lines.speaker: Councillor Andrews
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Lord Shiro Shayzien
lines.text: Take them away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something first.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Don't take too long. Let me know when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Well, look who it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to see you Martin.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Can you believe it? The coronation of a king! Truth be told, there were times I wondered if this day would ever come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And is it everything you hoped for?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Everything and more, my friend. And to think they even let us vote on it as well! Fantastic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the King certainly seems to have attracted plenty of guests.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Indeed. Seems that everyone wants to congratulate him... Or maybe they're just here for the food.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Either way, this will be an event for the ages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are all the leaders here as well?

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Most of them. Lord Shayzien was here earlier but he left. To be honest, he didn't look to be enjoying himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not too suprising. Things weren't exactly straightforward between him and the King when this was all arranged.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Yes, I heard there was a bit of drama here and there. Still, that's Kourend for you.

lines.speaker: Martin Holt
lines.text: Now, I'll let you go and mingle. I'm sure the King himself will want a word with you at some point as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Have fun Martin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lord Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Good to see you friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any word on Xamphur?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Nothing yet. However, we did manage to search his lair in Molch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Find anything interesting?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Most of it had been cleared out before we arrived. There was some research left behind though. Notes on potential new spells.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Some are far too dangerous to use, but others we've found to be safe. We're preparing a tome of all the ones that are suitable. I'll make sure you get a copy once we're done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very kind of you.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: It's the least we can do after all you have done for us. Anyway, I won't keep you. Enjoy the coronation.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: There you are! I was worried you'd miss all the fun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No chance. It's not every day you crown a new king.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You can say that again. You should have seen all the preperations needed. Food, drinks, entertainment, invitations... The list goes on.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: You'd think security would be one of the hard parts, but I actually reckon our bit is pretty straightforward in comparison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On that note, how does it feel to be serving a king now?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I don't think it's quite sunk in yet. It still feels like it was only this morning that Councillor Hughes escaped. So much has happened in such a short time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about it. Had I known how this would go when Martin first recruited me, I'd have probably considered running for the hills as fast as possible.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well, I for one am glad you didn't. We'd have never reached this point without your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wouldn't have been possible without you or Martin either. Have you two spoken yet?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Oh, I'm going to need a lot to drink before I'm ready for that conversation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Anyway, have you seen our special guest?

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: It's a pleasure to finally welcome you to Great Kourend, Queen Zyanyi. You are very welcome here.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: The pleasure is all mine. From today, the sun will shine on our two kingdoms once more. The mistakes of the past are now behind us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is she?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Zyanyi Arkan, the Sun Queen. She's the ruler of Varlamore, the kingdom in the south. This is her first visit to Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: First visit?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Indeed. The King personally invited her. He's keen to put an end to the animosity between the two kingdoms. Rumor has it she feels the same way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are the knights with her?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Her royal guard, the Kualti.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They look fancy.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: They do. If the stories are true, they're the best fighters in Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not jealous are you?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Not of that armour, that's for sure. It looks very uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Ah, looks like they're done. You should go and see the King. He was pretty keen to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to see you, Your Majesty. Congratulations on your coronation.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Thank you my friend. Of course, none of this would have been possible without you. Let it be known that you will always be a friend of this kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, I'm very honoured.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: As am I. In fact, I think this calls for a toast.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Gather around honoured guests! It's time for me to bore you all with a speech!

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Friends, thank you all for joining me on this special day. This is the beginning of a new chapter for Kourend, and I could not be more grateful to have you all here to celebrate with me.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Still, let us not forget the hard road that led us here, nor those who were brave enough to walk along it.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Martin Holt, Lydia Fullore, [player name]. Without you, we would all still be living in ignorance. You opened our eyes to the truth, and ensured a strong future for Kourend.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Now, let us all raise our glasses to these three. We are forever in their debt.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Ah, there you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wanted to speak to me?

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I did. Sorry to take you away from the festivities. With everyone else celebrating, I thought it would be a good chance to talk in private. Don't worry. It won't take long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem. What did you want to discuss?

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: The councillors are behind bars and Kourend has a new ruler. On the surface, all is well again. Of course, that's not quite the case in reality.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean Xamphur?

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Yes. The Council did not act alone. In fact, they weren't even the true orchestrators in this. We need to find and deal with the real foe before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd be happy to help in any way I can. Xamphur and I have unfinished business.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I was hoping you'd say that. This is a serious threat we're facing, and I need people I can trust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Nothing for now. I just wanted to make sure you were on board. Once we have more information on Xamphur, I will let you know.

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: Now, we should head back downstairs before we are missed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is everything alright, Your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Kandur Hosidius
lines.text: I... I can't...

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Kandur Hosidius. You have been marked for death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Lydia. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: It's been a rough few days since the coronation, as you can probably imagine. One moment we were crowning a new king, that next we were burying him. I'm doing okay, though. Work is a good distraction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that going?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Well Artur Hosidius is now the King of Great Kourend. I'm sorry you couldn't be there for the ceremony. After what happened, they felt something small would be best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fine. I think one coronation was enough for me. Any word on the killer?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: There's no shortage of potential suspects, but nothing solid. We've had the murder weapon sent over to Lovakengj to be examined. They have a theory, but want to do more work before they confirm it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well let me know once they have something. I want to find whoever did this.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: We will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else I can do in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: I don't think so. Lord Arceuus is trying to find out more about Xamphur, but he doesn't have anything yet. For now, all we can do is wait.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: And on that note, I wanted to give you a little something to thank you for all that you've done. Not just for Kourend, but for me as well.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: This is a bad situation we're in, but without you things would be a whole lot worse. Lord Arceuus also sent something over for you. He said you'd know what to do with it.

lines.speaker: Commander Fullore
lines.text: Now, I'll let you get going. Thanks again, for everything.
